name: Bronze Serpent Violent-Resolution Source Packet
purpose: Selected public-domain anchors for a Revelation action-horror room where a healing sign has become an idol, and the squad can resolve the anomaly through controlled violence against the object.

fragments.source_circumstance: The people speak against God and Moses; fiery serpents bite them; a bronze serpent set on a standard becomes the appointed means by which the bitten live when they look at it.
fragments.source_excerpt: Fiery serpents bite the people after complaint and rebellion. Moses makes a serpent of brass and sets it on a standard; those bitten look to it and live.
fragments.nightmare_room_seed: A modern therapeutic object saves bite victims only when watched, turning treatment into enforced attention. The cure is real, but the dependence is spiritually and physically dangerous.
fragments.escalation_thread: First the object heals, then it demands visible attention, then it punishes anyone who looks away, then it generates new bites to justify itself.
generation_rules: Make the healing function real enough that destroying the object is costly.
Use looking/attention as the symbolic mechanism.
Do not make this a generic snake monster scene; the danger is a corrupted remedy.

fragments.source_circumstance: Hezekiah breaks the bronze serpent Moses made because people have begun burning incense to it, calling it Nehushtan.
fragments.source_excerpt: Hezekiah removes high places, breaks pillars, cuts down the Asherah, and breaks in pieces the bronze serpent Moses made, because Israel had burned incense to it; he calls it Nehushtan.
fragments.nightmare_room_seed: A once-authorized medical artifact has become an object of devotion. The violent solution is to break the thing that used to save people.
fragments.escalation_thread: The squad must decide whether to breach and destroy the idol under contact, preserve it for study, or force a symbolic renaming that strips its authority.
generation_rules: The force option should be valid: breaking the object can close the anomaly locally.
The violent act must be against the idol/object, not patients or worshippers.
Name the moral problem: dependence on the sign has replaced the source of healing.

fragments.source_circumstance: Josiah removes idolatrous vessels, burns them, beats objects to dust, breaks altars and pillars, and defiles a place where children passed through fire.
fragments.source_excerpt: Objects made for false worship are brought out, burned, beaten to dust, and cast away. Altars and pillars are broken down; places of harmful ritual are defiled so the practice cannot continue.
fragments.nightmare_room_seed: A containment team may need to treat destruction as procedure: remove the object, neutralize the site, destroy ritual infrastructure, document the act, then manage fallout.
fragments.escalation_thread: If the object is only sealed, devotion continues around the seal. If it is studied too long, the site reorganizes around it.
generation_rules: Use controlled destruction as a procedural close condition.
Force should leave consequences: damaged equipment, contaminated dust, political complaint, or personnel stress.

fragments.source_circumstance: Ranger battle drills include entering and clearing a room; later text describes unit leader control, clearing teams, sectors, minimizing friendly casualties, and reporting rooms clear.
fragments.source_excerpt: Enter and clear a room is a battle drill. The unit clears and secures the room while minimizing friendly casualties; the leader controls security and clearing teams, sectors, and reports.
fragments.nightmare_room_seed: The object is in a treatment room or chapel-like clinic bay with patients nearby. The squad must breach, clear sectors, protect patients, and reach the object.
fragments.escalation_thread: A clean clear protects patients but slows the strike; a hard entry reaches the object faster but risks casualties and panic.
generation_rules: Make the violence tactical and controlled, not chaotic brawling.
Give Brooks or Owen a real close-quarters plan.

fragments.source_circumstance: The handbook includes demolitions and breaching charges used to break down barriers, plus safety and charge-placement considerations.
fragments.source_excerpt: Breaching charges are used to break down barriers. Demolition and breaching tasks require charge selection, placement, safety considerations, and follow-on clearing.
fragments.nightmare_room_seed: A destructive plan can be surgical: breach the case or support column holding the artifact, not the whole room. Failure produces shrapnel, dust, or sympathetic activation.
fragments.escalation_thread: Destroying the object resolves the rule quickly but risks artifact fragments and patient harm if the team misjudges the charge or containment wrap.
generation_rules: If using demolitions, keep it grounded: small charge, containment blanket, angle, line of sight, medical evacuation ready.
Make force one of multiple plans, not the only tone.

fragments.source_title: Judgment and Decision Making Under Stress: An Overview for Emergency Managers
fragments.source_circumstance: Emergency decision-making under stress can involve unclear warnings, faulty information, equipment failure, time pressure, and narrowed attention.
fragments.source_excerpt: Under emergency stress, warnings are often unclear, apparatus may fail, time pressure narrows attention, and people tend to focus on critical cues while neglecting alternatives.
fragments.nightmare_room_seed: The anomaly exploits narrowed attention: those who focus on the healing object survive the bite but lose broader awareness. Squad members under stress may fixate on it.
fragments.escalation_thread: A plan can fail because the officer cannot look away, misses a sector, or chooses preservation because the object is demonstrably healing someone.
generation_rules: Use attention and fixation as character-state pressure.
Show stress as narrowed focus, clipped communication, and missed cues.
